author: @Gwydion67
text:
Hi Luke!
Since no one mentioned yet:
Wow, you truely incorporated all of those tips for adjusting your level of expressed enthusiasm. At first I thought "hey, let Luke do his style, it's HIS video!". Now that you leveled it a bit, I have to admit that you DO come over a lot more natural. My respect, you did from one episode to the next what I couldn't achieve in years! Chapeau! 🎩

Oh, and not to forget:
Very nice light panel, a quite suitable project for the dark season... 🕯❄☃️🎄🥰👍
Warm greetings from south Germany! We already have snow falling...

author: @truthseeker3907
text: Thumbs up! 👍

author: @BrockGrimes
text: Luke,  Scuff the LED's lightly with a scuff pad, steel wool, etc. it'll defuse the light more.  I do this all the time with computer builds to get more glow and less pin point.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks for the tip, Cheers mate!

author: @DiyEcoProjects
text: *Hi Luke,* nice one bro, i like it. Did something similar ages ago when we organised a space at a festival. Image putting one of those light box signs on top of a 25ft geodome! was a pain in the arse... but looked great up there when it was fixed in place. old christmas lights running off 12v. Are you putting it outside the workshop? company logo with seasonal greetings? Also down poundland theres led strips running off 5v

author: @ThisRandomUsername
text: Nicely done Luke. If I'm not mistaken, Rob was experimenting with some one-dimensional Fresnel lenses a while ago with solar panels. If you were to put some of those directly over the LEDs, you might be able to make the light a bit more dispersed between the strips. You could also try mounting the LEDs sideways in the frame and put a bit of aluminium foil as a reflector from the back of the box. Probably not worth the effort for a small project like that which already looks great.

author: @garethb5729
text:
Hi Luke. Great work, really enjoyable. Thank you. 

Any chance on a small build with a thrust bearing. Just because everyone knows of skate bearings. 

Keep up the good work.

author: @NorthernKitty
text: What a great idea!!  You might consider framing the stencils and then making a slot to hold the frame, so you can easily swap out stencils for different occasions.  Or maybe I'm getting ahead of your next video? 😊  I already have a light panel, but hadn't considered stencils as you've shown - thanks so much for the inspiration, Luke!!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Awesome! The next part going to be something else lol. But it's definitely worth doing your own stencils because they look so cool. Thanks mate! :)

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: I've used overhead projector pens to colourize plain car bulbs for indicators,passed mot lol! Probably good for led lights  through a diffused layer,I've also used glass paint made by vittrail,not sure if it's correctly spelt? They also make tubes of a substance that stands raised off the glass,bit like stained glass leading:)

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
